findings.description: The infographic image from page 1 is missing in the HTML conversion. The HTML renders the content as structured text with bullet points instead of the visual infographic with circular/oval design elements, icons, and visual hierarchy.
findings.evidence: PDF shows an elaborate infographic with three main sections (Guidelines, Printing, Before Sending) in stylized oval/pill shapes with icons (book, printer, envelope, documents). HTML renders this as plain text headings and bullet points.

findings.description: Page 1 infographic's three-column layout (Guidelines left, Printing center, Before Sending right) is collapsed into a single-column sequential layout in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows three parallel sections arranged spatially around a central circle. HTML shows them stacked vertically as sequential sections.

findings.description: Page 2 (GAS-1259 form) loses its multi-column layout for the header area. The PDF has fields arranged in a complex grid (Trade Name, Street Address/County side by side, contact fields with phone/fax, and a right-side panel with checkboxes, Account Number, and Return for Month of). The HTML renders these as a single-column sequential list.
findings.evidence: PDF: Street Address and County fields are side by side; City, State, and Zip Code are on one row; Fill in applicable circles box, Account Number, and Return for Month are in a right-side panel. HTML: All fields are listed vertically one after another.

findings.description: In the Part 1 table, the grayed-out cells for Dyed Diesel and Dyed Kerosene columns in certain rows (lines 1, 8 in Part 2) that indicate non-applicable fields are not visually distinguished in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows gray shading in certain cells to indicate they are not applicable. HTML shows empty cells without visual distinction.

findings.description: The dollar sign ($) indicator in the Total column of line 7 appears as a standalone '$' character in the HTML rather than being positioned within the cell as shown in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a large '$' symbol in the Total column for line 7. HTML shows '$' but in a different visual presentation.

findings.description: In Part 2 Gallonage Summary, the grayed-out cells (Gasoline column for lines 8, 9, 11; Dyed Diesel and Dyed Kerosene for lines 10, 12) are not visually indicated in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF uses gray shading to indicate non-applicable cells. HTML shows all cells as empty white cells.

findings.description: The signature block layout is different. PDF has Signature, Title, and Date on a single line. HTML has them stacked with Date appearing on a separate line.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Signature: _____ Title: _____ Date: _____' on one line. HTML: Signature and Title on one line, Date on the next.

findings.description: The MAIL TO and QUESTIONS sections are arranged side by side in the PDF but stacked vertically in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF: MAIL TO on left, QUESTIONS on right. HTML: MAIL TO followed by QUESTIONS below.

findings.description: Multiple h1 elements used. 'Do Not Include This Page' is h1, and 'Motor Fuel Backup Tax Return' is also h1. The document should have only one h1.
findings.evidence:
HTML has

Do Not Include This Page

in Page 1 section and

Motor Fuel Backup Tax Return

in Page 2 section.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is broken. Under the page 2 section, h2 'GAS-1259' appears before h1 'Motor Fuel Backup Tax Return', and h3 elements like 'Fill in applicable circles:', 'Account Number', 'Return for Month of' are used for form field labels rather than document sections.
findings.evidence:
HTML:

GAS-1259

followed by

Motor Fuel Backup Tax Return

. Also

Fill in applicable circles:

,

Account Number

,

Return for Month of

used for form labels.

findings.description: The nested <main> elements create structural duplication. There is an outer <main> and an inner <main> element.

In Part 1, Line 1 for 'Motor fuel road tax due', the 'Jet Fuel and AvGas' column should be shaded/greyed out (not applicable) per the PDF, but the HTML renders it as a regular empty cell indistinguishable from fillable cells. Similarly, Part 2 has several greyed-out cells for certain fuel types on certain lines that are not distinguished in HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows grey/shaded cells for Line 1 Jet Fuel and AvGas column, and Part 2 Lines 8-11 have various greyed columns for Gasoline, Undyed Diesel, Undyed Kerosene, and Jet Fuel and AvGas. HTML does not mark these as distinct.

findings.description: One image has empty alt text, which is not descriptive.
findings.evidence: HTML:
